title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: article demonstrates restricted contiguous-motif workflow gp3sequences. workflow accepts ordinary long-format data frames require Gazepoint software, hardware, exports, gp3tools. functions describe recurring state windows locations. infer attention, cognition, emotion, intention, psychological state, causal mechanisms.

title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: example contains three sequences one preserved grouping variable.
code: library(gp3sequences)  sequence_data <- data.frame(   sequence = c(     rep("s1", 5L),     rep("s2", 4L),     rep("s3", 3L)   ),   order = c(1:5, 1:4, 1:3),   state = c(     "A", "B", "A", "B", "C",     "A", "B", "C", "B",     "B", "A", "B"   ),   group = c(     rep("g1", 5L),     rep("g1", 4L),     rep("g2", 3L)   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  sequence_data #>    sequence order state group #> 1        s1     1     A    g1 #> 2        s1     2     B    g1 #> 3        s1     3     A    g1 #> 4        s1     4     B    g1 #> 5        s1     5     C    g1 #> 6        s2     1     A    g1 #> 7        s2     2     B    g1 #> 8        s2     3     C    g1 #> 9        s2     4     B    g1 #> 10       s3     1     B    g2 #> 11       s3     2     A    g2 #> 12       s3     3     B    g2

title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: extract_sequence_ngrams() enumerates contiguous state windows . Minimum maximum motif lengths overlapping-occurrence policy explicit.
code: extracted <- extract_sequence_ngrams(   sequence_data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence",   order_col = "order",   state_col = "state",   metadata_cols = "group",   min_length = 2L,   max_length = 3L,   overlap = "allow" )  head(extracted$occurrences) #>   sequence_id group    motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length start_index #> 1          s1    g1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2           1 #> 2          s1    g1 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3           1 #> 3          s1    g1    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2           2 #> 4          s1    g1 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3           2 #> 5          s1    g1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2           3 #> 6          s1    g1 L3:S1|S2|S3  S1|S2|S3 A > B > C            3           3 #>   end_index start_order end_order start_original_row end_original_row #> 1         2           1         2                  1                2 #> 2         3           1         3                  1                3 #> 3         3           2         3                  2                3 #> 4         4           2         4                  2                4 #> 5         4           3         4                  3                4 #> 6         5           3         5                  3                5 #>   occurrence_index #> 1                1 #> 2                1 #> 3                1 #> 4                1 #> 5                2 #> 6                1

title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: Sequence prevalence uses every validated sequence denominator. Filtering deterministic retains explicit thresholds tie handling.
code: motif_summary <- summarise_sequence_motifs(extracted)  motif_filter <- filter_sequence_motifs(   motif_summary,   min_occurrences = 2L,   min_sequences = 2L,   min_prevalence = 0.50,   motif_lengths = c(2L, 3L),   top_n = 10L,   rank_by = "sequence_prevalence",   ties = "include" )  motif_table <- format_sequence_motifs(   motif_filter,   prevalence = "percent",   digits = 1L )  motif_table$table #>   rank    motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length n_occurrences n_sequences #> 1    1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2             4           3 #> 2    2    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2             2           2 #> 3    2    L2:S2|S3     S2|S3     B > C            2             2           2 #> 4    2 L3:S1|S2|S3  S1|S2|S3 A > B > C            3             2           2 #> 5    2 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3             2           2 #>   sequence_prevalence_percent occurrence_share_percent #> 1                       100.0                     26.7 #> 2                        66.7                     13.3 #> 3                        66.7                     13.3 #> 4                        66.7                     13.3 #> 5                        66.7                     13.3 #>   mean_occurrences_per_sequence mean_occurrences_when_present #> 1                           1.3                           1.3 #> 2                           0.7                           1.0 #> 3                           0.7                           1.0 #> 4                           0.7                           1.0 #> 5                           0.7                           1.0

title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: Positions may represent start, centre, end motif occurrence. Absolute positions use one-based state indices. Relative positions range 0 1 across sequence. format_sequence_motif_positions() changes display precision units without modifying underlying analytical object.
code: position_summary <- summarise_sequence_motif_positions(   extracted,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative",   by = "group" )  position_summary$summary #>    group    motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length position_basis #> 1     g1 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3         centre #> 2     g1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2         centre #> 3     g1    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2         centre #> 4     g1 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3         centre #> 5     g1 L3:S1|S2|S3  S1|S2|S3 A > B > C            3         centre #> 6     g1 L3:S2|S3|S2  S2|S3|S2 B > C > B            3         centre #> 7     g1    L2:S2|S3     S2|S3     B > C            2         centre #> 8     g1    L2:S3|S2     S3|S2     C > B            2         centre #> 9     g2    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2         centre #> 10    g2 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3         centre #> 11    g2    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2         centre #>    position_scale n_occurrences n_sequences min_position max_position #> 1        relative             1           1    0.2500000    0.2500000 #> 2        relative             3           2    0.1250000    0.6250000 #> 3        relative             1           1    0.3750000    0.3750000 #> 4        relative             1           1    0.5000000    0.5000000 #> 5 0.1666667 #> 3      0.3750000       0.3750000 #> 4      0.5000000       0.5000000 #> 5      0.5416667       0.5416667 #> 6      0.6666667       0.6666667 #> 7      0.6875000       0.6875000 #> 8      0.8333333       0.8333333 #> 9      0.2500000       0.2500000 #> 10     0.5000000       0.5000000 #> 11     0.7500000       0.7500000 position_table <- format_sequence_motif_positions(   position_summary,   digits = 1L,   position_units = "percent",   include_rank = TRUE )  position_table$table #>    group rank    motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length position_basis #> 1     g1    1 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3         centre #> 2     g1    2    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2         centre #> 3     g1    3    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2         centre #> 4     g1    4 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3         centre #> 5     g1    5 L3:S1|S2|S3  S1|S2|S3 A > B > C            3         centre #> 6     g1    6 L3:S2|S3|S2  S2|S3|S2 B > C > B            3         centre #> 7     g1    7    L2:S2|S3     S2|S3     B > C            2         centre #> 8     g1    8    L2:S3|S2     S3|S2     C > B            2         centre #> 9     g2    1    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2         centre #> 10    g2    2 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3         centre #> 11    g2    3    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2         centre #>    position_scale position_unit n_occurrences n_sequences min_position #> 1        relative       percent             1           1         25.0 #> 2        relative percent             1           1         50.0 #> 11       relative       percent             1           1         75.0 #>    max_position mean_position median_position #> 1          25.0          25.0            25.0 #> 2          62.5          30.6            16.7 #> 3          37.5          37.5            37.5 #> 4          50.0          50.0            50.0 #> 5          75.0          54.2 title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: plot_sequence_motifs() uses base R graphics. returned data frame contains exact motifs values used plot. Sequence prevalence frequent contiguous motifs.
code: plotted_motifs <- plot_sequence_motifs(   motif_summary,   metric = "sequence_prevalence",   top_n = 8L,   motif_lengths = c(2L, 3L),   ties = "include",   horizontal = TRUE ) plotted_motifs[   c("plot_rank", "motif", "motif_length", "plot_value") ] #>   plot_rank     motif motif_length plot_value #> 1         1     A > B            2  1.0000000 #> 2         2     B > A            2  0.6666667 #> 3         3     B > C            2  0.6666667 #> 4         4 A > B > C            3  0.6666667 #> 5         5 B > A > B            3  0.6666667 #> 6         6     C > B            2  0.3333333 #> 7         7 A > B > A            3  0.3333333 #> 8         8 B > C > B            3  0.3333333

title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: strip display shows individual occurrence positions deterministic stacking. random jitter used. Relative centre positions selected motif occurrences. distribution display provides compact base-R summary structural positions. Distributions relative motif centre positions.
code: strip_data <- plot_sequence_motif_positions(   extracted,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative",   top_n = 5L,   display = "strip" ) head(   strip_data[     c(       "sequence_id",       "motif",       "position_value",       "plot_rank"     )   ] ) #>   sequence_id motif position_value plot_rank #> 1          s1 A > B      0.1250000         1 #> 2          s2 A > B      0.1666667         1 #> 3          s1 A > B      0.6250000         1 #> 4          s3 A > B      0.7500000         1 #> 5          s3 B > A      0.2500000         2 #> 6          s1 B > A      0.3750000         2 plot_sequence_motif_positions(   extracted,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative",   top_n = 5L,   display = "distribution" )

title: Contiguous Motif Workflow
text: reported counts, prevalence values, positions describe supplied ordered states declared preparation, motif-length, overlap, filtering, position rules. substantive interpretation belongs research design inferred automatically motif structure alone.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Stefanos Balaskas. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Balaskas S (2026). gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis Ordered Categorical Sequences. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3sequences/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis of Ordered Categorical Sequences},   author = {Stefanos Balaskas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3sequences/}, }

title: gp3sequences
text: gp3sequences independent R package transparent, reproducible, auditable analysis ordered categorical sequences. designed ordinary long-format data frames restricted Gazepoint exports, eye-tracking data, particular hardware, proprietary software.

title: gp3sequences
text: package initial development yet provide stable public analytical API. first development series prioritises neutral data contract, explicit preprocessing policies, machine-readable diagnostics, deterministic structural summaries.

title: gp3sequences
text: Potential applications include: eye-tracking AOI scanpaths; website-navigation customer-journey sequences; behavioural-state task-action sequences; interface-interaction sequences; educational activity sequences; repeated ordered categorical data.

title: gp3sequences
text: core data contract require configurable columns corresponding sequence_id, sequence_order, state. Optional participant, trial, condition, group, timing, duration, weight, metadata columns may also mapped explicitly. minimal input may look like :
code: example_sequences <- data.frame(   sequence_id = rep(c("s1", "s2"), each = 4L),   sequence_order = rep(1:4, times = 2L),   state = c(     "home", "search", "product", "checkout",     "home", "category", "product", "home"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  example_sequences #>   sequence_id sequence_order    state #> 1          s1              1     home #> 2          s1              2   search #> 3          s1              3  product #> 4          s1              4 checkout #> 5          s2              1     home #> 6          s2              2 category #> 7          s2              3  product #> 8          s2              4     home

title: gp3sequences
text: initial public API provides three related functions: audit_sequence_data() reports structured data-quality issues; validate_sequence_data() returns compact validity contract; prepare_sequence_data() applies explicit preprocessing policies creates deterministic canonical sequence data.
code: audit <- audit_sequence_data(   example_sequences,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  validation <- validate_sequence_data(   example_sequences,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  prepared <- prepare_sequence_data(   example_sequences,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state",   repeated_state_policy = "preserve" )

title: gp3sequences
text: Related functions support deterministic encoding descriptive sequence summaries without assigning substantive meaning states: encode_sequence_data() creates transparent state dictionary; summarise_sequence_states() reports state frequencies durations; summarise_sequence_transitions() reports adjacent transition counts; format_sequence_paths() creates compact ordered path strings.
code: encoded <- encode_sequence_data(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  state_summary <- summarise_sequence_states(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  transition_summary <- summarise_sequence_transitions(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  paths <- format_sequence_paths(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )

title: gp3sequences
text: motif workflow enumerates contiguous state windows retains explicit structural definitions: extract_sequence_ngrams() extracts auditable motif occurrences; summarise_sequence_motifs() reports counts sequence prevalence; filter_sequence_motifs() applies explicit transparent thresholds; format_sequence_motifs() creates stable report-ready tables.
code: motif_occurrences <- extract_sequence_ngrams(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state",   min_length = 2,   max_length = 4,   overlap = "allow" )  motif_summary <- summarise_sequence_motifs(motif_occurrences)  motif_filter <- filter_sequence_motifs(   motif_summary,   min_sequences = 2,   min_prevalence = 0.10,   top_n = 20,   ties = "include" )  motif_table <- format_sequence_motifs(   motif_filter,   prevalence = "percent",   digits = 1 )

title: gp3sequences
text: Motif occurrences can summarised plotted without adding analytical dependencies: summarise_sequence_motif_positions() reports structural locations; format_sequence_motif_positions() produces explicit report tables; plot_sequence_motifs() plots prevalence occurrence metrics; plot_sequence_motif_positions() plots occurrence locations.
code: position_summary <- summarise_sequence_motif_positions(   motif_occurrences,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative" )  position_table <- format_sequence_motif_positions(   position_summary,   position_units = "percent",   digits = 1 )  plot_sequence_motifs(   motif_summary,   metric = "sequence_prevalence",   top_n = 10 )  plot_sequence_motif_positions(   motif_occurrences,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative",   top_n = 5 )

title: gp3sequences
text: Sequence outputs describe behavioural structural patterns . independently establish emotion, cognition, comprehension, personality, intention, diagnosis, deception, psychological attributes. Substantive interpretation requires appropriate study design external evidence.

title: gp3sequences
text: Stefanos Balaskas ORCID · Personal website · LinkedIn

title: gp3sequences
text: gp3sequences released MIT License.

title: Audit Long-Format Sequence Data — audit_sequence_data
text: Examines long-format data frame neutral gp3sequences sequence-data contract without modifying input.

title: Audit Long-Format Sequence Data — audit_sequence_data
code: audit_sequence_data(   data,   sequence_id_col,   order_col,   state_col,   duration_col = NULL,   metadata_cols = NULL,   expected_states = NULL )

title: Audit Long-Format Sequence Data — audit_sequence_data
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. duration_col Optional name numeric duration column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values.

title: Audit Long-Format Sequence Data — audit_sequence_data
text: data frame one row per detected issue stable columns sequence_id, row, column, issue_code, severity, value, message, action. Severity values error, review, info.

title: Audit Long-Format Sequence Data — audit_sequence_data
text: audit checks column mappings, empty inputs, missing identifiers, missing non-numeric order values, duplicated positions, integer order gaps, unordered rows, missing states, consecutive repeated states, single-row sequences, invalid durations, inconsistent metadata, unexpected states, unused factor levels. function reports structural properties . infer psychological, cognitive, emotional, diagnostic states.

title: Audit Long-Format Sequence Data — audit_sequence_data
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = rep(c("s1", "s2"), each = 3L),   position = rep(1:3, times = 2L),   state = c("home", "search", "product", "home", "category", "product") )  audit_sequence_data(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state" ) #> [1] sequence_id row         column      issue_code  severity    value       #> [7] message     action      #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

title: Encode Ordered Sequence States — encode_sequence_data
text: Creates deterministic dictionary adds integer labelled state codes long-format sequence data.

title: Encode Ordered Sequence States — encode_sequence_data
code: encode_sequence_data(   data,   sequence_id_col,   order_col,   state_col,   duration_col = NULL,   metadata_cols = NULL,   expected_states = NULL,   state_levels = NULL,   prefix = "S",   width = NULL )

title: Encode Ordered Sequence States — encode_sequence_data
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. duration_col Optional name numeric duration column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values. state_levels Optional atomic vector defining complete state ordering. omitted, factor levels respected; otherwise observed state labels sorted alphabetically. prefix Character prefix used labelled codes. width Optional positive integer width numeric part labelled code. default determined dictionary size.

title: Encode Ordered Sequence States — encode_sequence_data
text: named list containing: data: deterministically sorted canonical data state_index state_code; dictionary: state labels, integer indices, labelled codes, observed-state indicator; audit, status, mapping input validation; settings: resolved code prefix width.

title: Encode Ordered Sequence States — encode_sequence_data
text: function reinterpret states. State codes transparent identifiers derived explicit deterministic state ordering.

title: Encode Ordered Sequence States — encode_sequence_data
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = c("s1", "s1", "s2", "s2"),   position = c(1, 2, 1, 2),   state = c("home", "search", "home", "product") )  encoded <- encode_sequence_data(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state" )  encoded$dictionary #>     state state_index state_code observed #> 1    home           1         S1     TRUE #> 2 product           2         S2     TRUE #> 3  search           3         S3     TRUE encoded$data #>   sequence_id sequence_order   state state_index state_code original_row #> 1          s1              1    home           1         S1            1 #> 2          s1              2  search           3         S3            2 #> 3          s2              1    home           1         S1            3 #> 4          s2              2 product           2         S2            4

title: Extract Contiguous Sequence N-Grams — extract_sequence_ngrams
text: Enumerates contiguous state motifs validated long-format sequence data.

title: Extract Contiguous Sequence N-Grams — extract_sequence_ngrams
code: extract_sequence_ngrams(   data,   sequence_id_col,   order_col,   state_col,   duration_col = NULL,   metadata_cols = NULL,   expected_states = NULL,   min_length = 2L,   max_length = 3L,   overlap = c("allow", "disallow"),   separator = " > ",   state_levels = NULL )

title: Extract Contiguous Sequence N-Grams — extract_sequence_ngrams
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. duration_col Optional name numeric duration column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values. min_length Positive whole number giving shortest motif length. max_length Positive whole number giving longest motif length. overlap Character value specifying whether overlapping occurrences motif within sequence "allow"ed "disallow"ed. separator Character value used display state labels human-readable motif column. state_levels Optional atomic vector defining complete state ordering. omitted, factor levels respected; otherwise observed labels sorted deterministically.

title: Extract Contiguous Sequence N-Grams — extract_sequence_ngrams
text: named list containing: occurrences: one row per retained contiguous motif occurrence; motifs: distinct motif dictionary; sequences: sequence-level counts candidate retained occurrences; state_dictionary: deterministic state dictionary; audit, status, mapping input validation; settings: resolved motif extraction settings.

title: Extract Contiguous Sequence N-Grams — extract_sequence_ngrams
text: Motifs contiguous windows . subsequence gaps, edit distances, statistical tests, substantive interpretations introduced. Consecutive repeated states used exactly supplied; repeat collapsing performed explicitly prepare_sequence_data() extraction. overlap = "disallow", overlap resolved independently within sequence-motif pair deterministic left--right greedy rule. Different motifs different motif lengths compete positions. collision-resistant motif_id motif_key columns derived deterministic state codes. display separator may therefore also occur inside state label without changing motif identity.

title: Extract Contiguous Sequence N-Grams — extract_sequence_ngrams
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = c(rep("s1", 5L), rep("s2", 4L)),   position = c(1:5, 1:4),   state = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "A", "A", "B", "A", "C") )  ngrams <- extract_sequence_ngrams(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state",   min_length = 2,   max_length = 3,   overlap = "allow" )  ngrams$occurrences #>    sequence_id    motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length start_index #> 1           s1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2           1 #> 2           s1 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3           1 #> 3           s1    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2           2 #> 4           s1 L3:S2|S1|S2  S2|S1|S2 B > A > B            3           2 #> 5           s1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2           3 #> 6           s1 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3           3 #> 7           s1    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2           4 #> 8           s2    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2           1 #> 9           s2 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3           1 #> 10          s2    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2           2 #> 11          s2 L3:S2|S1|S3  S2|S1|S3 B > A > C            3           2 #> 12          s2    L2:S1|S3     S1|S3     A > C            2           3 #>    end_index start_order end_order start_original_row end_original_row #> 1          2           1         2                  1                2 #> 2          3           1         3                  1                3 #> 3          3           2         3                  2                3 #> 4          4           2         4                  2                4 #> 5          4           3         4                  3                4 #> 6          5           3         5                  3                5 #> 7          5           4         5                  4                5 #> 8          2           1         2                  6                7 #> 9          3           1         3                  6                8 #> 10         3           2         3                  7                8 #> 11         4           2         4                  7                9 #> 12         4           3         4                  8                9 #>    occurrence_index #> 1                 1 #> 2                 1 #> 3                 1 #> 4 ngrams$motifs #>      motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length #> 1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2 #> 2    L2:S1|S3     S1|S3     A > C            2 #> 3    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A title: Filter Sequence Motif Summaries — filter_sequence_motifs
text: Applies transparent count, prevalence, length, top-ranking filters sequence motif summaries.

title: Filter Sequence Motif Summaries — filter_sequence_motifs
code: filter_sequence_motifs(   x,   min_occurrences = 1L,   min_sequences = 1L,   min_prevalence = 0,   motif_lengths = NULL,   top_n = NULL,   rank_by = c("sequence_prevalence", "n_occurrences", "n_sequences"),   ties = c("include", "first") )

title: Filter Sequence Motif Summaries — filter_sequence_motifs
text: x motif extraction, motif summary, filtered-motif object. min_occurrences Non-negative whole number giving minimum total occurrence count. min_sequences Non-negative whole number giving minimum number sequences containing motif. min_prevalence Minimum sequence prevalence 0 1. motif_lengths Optional vector positive whole-number motif lengths retain. top_n Optional positive whole number giving requested number highest-ranked motifs. rank_by Metric used deterministic top-ranking: one "sequence_prevalence", "n_occurrences", "n_sequences". ties Character value controlling top-n boundary. "include" retains every motif tied rank_by final selected motif; "first" retains exactly top_n motifs deterministic secondary sorting.

title: Filter Sequence Motif Summaries — filter_sequence_motifs
text: named list containing filtered motifs table, matching sequence-level rows by_sequence, sequence state dictionaries, validation metadata, filter settings, counts filtering.

title: Filter Sequence Motif Summaries — filter_sequence_motifs
text: Filtering descriptive deterministic. ties = "first", ties resolved sequence prevalence, occurrence count, sequence count, shorter motif length, finally collision-resistant motif key.

title: Filter Sequence Motif Summaries — filter_sequence_motifs
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = c(rep("s1", 5L), rep("s2", 4L)),   position = c(1:5, 1:4),   state = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "A", "A", "B", "A", "C") )  extracted <- extract_sequence_ngrams(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state" )  filtered <- filter_sequence_motifs(   extracted,   min_sequences = 2,   top_n = 5,   ties = "include" )  filtered$motifs #>      motif_id motif_key     motif motif_length n_occurrences n_sequences #> 1    L2:S1|S2     S1|S2     A > B            2             3           2 #> 2    L2:S2|S1     S2|S1     B > A            2             3           2 #> 3 L3:S1|S2|S1  S1|S2|S1 A > B > A            3             3           2 #>   sequence_prevalence occurrence_share mean_occurrences_per_sequence #> 1                   1             0.25                           1.5 #> 2                   1             0.25                           1.5 #> 3                   1             0.25                           1.5 #>   mean_occurrences_when_present #> 1                           1.5 #> 2                           1.5 #> 3                           1.5

what: gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis of Ordered Categorical Sequences — gp3sequences-package
title: gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis of Ordered Categorical Sequences — gp3sequences-package
text: gp3sequences provides transparent, reproducible, auditable tools working ordered categorical sequences represented ordinary long-format data frames.

title: gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis of Ordered Categorical Sequences — gp3sequences-package
text: package designed around explicit column mappings, deterministic ordering, documented preprocessing policies, machine-readable diagnostics, conservative structural summaries. restricted eye-tracking data particular device export format.

title: gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis of Ordered Categorical Sequences — gp3sequences-package
text: Sequence outputs describe behavioural structural patterns. independently establish emotion, cognition, comprehension, personality, intention, diagnosis, deception, psychological attributes. Substantive interpretation requires appropriate study design external evidence.

title: gp3sequences: Transparent Analysis of Ordered Categorical Sequences — gp3sequences-package
text: Maintainer: Stefanos Balaskas s.balaskas@ac.upatras.gr (ORCID) Authors: Stefanos Balaskas s.balaskas@ac.upatras.gr (ORCID)

title: Prepare Long-Format Sequence Data — prepare_sequence_data
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. duration_col Optional name numeric duration column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values. missing_state_policy Policy missing states: "error" "drop". duplicate_position_policy Policy duplicated sequence positions: "error", "first", "last". repeated_state_policy Policy consecutive repeated states: "preserve" "collapse". zero_duration_policy Policy zero durations: "preserve", "drop", "error". unknown_state_policy Policy states absent expected_states: "preserve", "drop", "error". unused_state_levels Policy unused factor levels: "preserve" "drop".

Unmapped columns preserved.

title: Prepare Long-Format Sequence Data — prepare_sequence_data

> A > B            3             1           1 #> 6 L3:S2|S1|S3  S2|S1|S3 B > A > C            3             1           1 #>   sequence_prevalence occurrence_share mean_occurrences_per_sequence #> 1                 1.0       0.25000000                           1.5 #> 2                 1.0       0.25000000                           1.5 #> 3                 1.0       0.25000000                           1.5 #> 4                 0.5       0.08333333                           0.5 #> 5                 0.5       0.08333333                           0.5 #> 6                 0.5       0.08333333                           0.5 #>   mean_occurrences_when_present #> 1                           1.5 #> 2                           1.5 #> 3                           1.5 #> 4                           1.0 #> 5                           1.0 #> 6                           1.0

title: Summarise Sequence States — summarise_sequence_states
text: Produces per-sequence overall state-frequency summaries validated ordered sequence data.

title: Summarise Sequence States — summarise_sequence_states
code: summarise_sequence_states(   data,   sequence_id_col,   order_col,   state_col,   duration_col = NULL,   metadata_cols = NULL,   expected_states = NULL )

title: Summarise Sequence States — summarise_sequence_states
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. duration_col Optional name numeric duration column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values.

title: Summarise Sequence States — summarise_sequence_states
text: named list containing: by_sequence: state counts proportions within sequence; overall: state counts proportions across sequences; audit, status, mapping input validation. duration_col supplied, tables also include duration sums, duration proportions, mean durations.

title: Summarise Sequence States — summarise_sequence_states
text: Observation proportions use state rows denominator. Sequence proportions report proportion sequences state occurs. Missing durations excluded duration calculations; -missing duration group returns NA.

title: Summarise Sequence States — summarise_sequence_states
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = c("s1", "s1", "s1", "s2", "s2"),   position = c(1, 2, 3, 1, 2),   state = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "C") )  summaries <- summarise_sequence_states(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state" )  summaries$by_sequence #>   sequence_id state n_observations observation_proportion #> 1          s1     A              2              0.6666667 #> 2          s1     B              1              0.3333333 #> 3          s2     B              1              0.5000000 #> 4          s2     C              1              0.5000000 summaries$overall #>   state n_sequences sequence_proportion n_observations observation_proportion #> 1     A           1                 0.5              2                    0.4 #> 2     B           2                 1.0              2                    0.4 #> 3     C           1                 0.5              1                    0.2

title: Summarise Adjacent Sequence Transitions — summarise_sequence_transitions
text: Counts transitions adjacent ordered states sequence across complete data set.

title: Summarise Adjacent Sequence Transitions — summarise_sequence_transitions
code: summarise_sequence_transitions(   data,   sequence_id_col,   order_col,   state_col,   metadata_cols = NULL,   expected_states = NULL,   include_self = TRUE )

title: Summarise Adjacent Sequence Transitions — summarise_sequence_transitions
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values. include_self Logical value indicating whether transitions state state included.

title: Summarise Adjacent Sequence Transitions — summarise_sequence_transitions
text: named list containing: by_sequence: transition counts, proportions within sequence, conditional proportions within origin state; overall: transition counts, sequence coverage, global proportions, conditional origin-state proportions; audit, status, mapping, resolved include_self setting.

title: Summarise Adjacent Sequence Transitions — summarise_sequence_transitions
text: transition defined adjacent rows deterministic ordering sequence identifier, sequence order, original row. Sequences one state contribute transitions.

title: Summarise Adjacent Sequence Transitions — summarise_sequence_transitions
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = c("s1", "s1", "s1", "s2", "s2"),   position = c(1, 2, 3, 1, 2),   state = c("A", "B", "C", "A", "C") )  transitions <- summarise_sequence_transitions(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state" )  transitions$by_sequence #>   sequence_id from_state to_state n_transitions sequence_transition_proportion #> 1          s1          A        B             1                            0.5 #> 2          s1          B        C             1                            0.5 #> 3          s2          A        C             1                            1.0 #>   origin_transition_proportion #> 1                            1 #> 2                            1 #> 3                            1 transitions$overall #>   from_state to_state n_sequences sequence_proportion n_transitions #> 1          A        B           1                 0.5             1 #> 2          B        C           1                 0.5             1 #> 3          A        C           1                 0.5             1 #>   transition_proportion origin_transition_proportion #> 1             0.3333333                          0.5 #> 2             0.3333333                          1.0 #> 3             0.3333333                          0.5

title: Validate Long-Format Sequence Data — validate_sequence_data
text: Produces compact validation result based audit_sequence_data() without modifying input.

title: Validate Long-Format Sequence Data — validate_sequence_data
code: validate_sequence_data(   data,   sequence_id_col,   order_col,   state_col,   duration_col = NULL,   metadata_cols = NULL,   expected_states = NULL )

title: Validate Long-Format Sequence Data — validate_sequence_data
text: data data frame containing ordered state observations. sequence_id_col Name sequence identifier column. order_col Name numeric sequence-order column. state_col Name categorical state column. duration_col Optional name numeric duration column. metadata_cols Optional character vector naming columns remain constant within sequence. expected_states Optional vector known permitted state values.

title: Validate Long-Format Sequence Data — validate_sequence_data
text: named list containing valid, status, issue counts, complete audit table, column mapping, row sequence counts, observed state_levels. result valid error-severity issue present. Review-severity issues automatically invalidate input.

title: Validate Long-Format Sequence Data — validate_sequence_data
code: sequences <- data.frame(   id = rep(c("s1", "s2"), each = 2L),   position = rep(1:2, times = 2L),   state = c("home", "search", "home", "product") )  validation <- validate_sequence_data(   sequences,   sequence_id_col = "id",   order_col = "position",   state_col = "state" )  validation$status #> [1] "pass" validation$valid #> [1] TRUE

title: gp3sequences 0.0.0.9000
text: Created independent gp3sequences package scaffold. Added MIT licensing package-level documentation. Added initial testthat edition 3 infrastructure. Defined initial neutral scope interpretation boundary. Added initial README pkgdown website configuration. Added multi-platform GitHub Actions R CMD check workflow macOS, Windows, Ubuntu release, Ubuntu devel. Added GitHub Actions pkgdown workflow automated website building deployment. Added audit_sequence_data() validate_sequence_data() structured input diagnostics. Added prepare_sequence_data() explicit missing-state, duplicate-position, repeated-state, duration, unknown-state policies. Added encode_sequence_data() deterministic state dictionaries transparent state codes. Added summarise_sequence_states(), summarise_sequence_transitions(), format_sequence_paths() structural sequence summaries. Added extract_sequence_ngrams() deterministic contiguous motif enumeration explicit overlap handling. Added summarise_sequence_motifs(), filter_sequence_motifs(), format_sequence_motifs() transparent motif prevalence, filtering, reporting. Added summarise_sequence_motif_positions() format_sequence_motif_positions() deterministic structural location summaries. Added dependency-free plot_sequence_motifs() plot_sequence_motif_positions() visualisations plus synthetic contiguous-motif workflow article.
